description: Numista provides a REST API to access the numismatic catalogue and collection data programmatically. The functionalities include coin search, coin identification by image, coin pricing, and coin collection management.

tools.name: catalogues_get
tools.description: Retrieve the list of all the reference catalogues used for cross-reference in the catalogue. Returns the CataloguesGetResponse.

tools.name: issuers_get
tools.description: Retrieve the details about all the issuing countries and territories. Optional: lang (default: en). Returns the IssuersGetResponse.

tools.name: mints_get
tools.description: Retrieve the details about all the mints. Optional: lang (default: en). Returns the MintsGetResponse.

tools.name: mints_get-mintid
tools.description: Retrieve the details about a specific mint. Required: mint_id. Optional: lang (default: en). Returns the Mint.

tools.name: oauth-token_get
tools.description: In order to access the data of a Numista user, you will need to authenticate using the OAuth 2.0 protocol. See the section 'Authentication' above. Two types of authentications are available: authorization code and client credentials. For the 'authoriation code' flow, call the endpoint `/oauth_token` with the following parameters: - `grant_type` is 'authorization_code'. - `code` is the code you received in the query string of the back redirection described above. - `client_id` is the client ID which was assigned to your application and provided together with your API key. - `client_secret` is your API key. - `redirect_uri` is the redirection URI you specified for the step described above. For the 'client credentials' flow, call the endpoint `/oauth_token` with the following parameters: - `grant_type` is 'client_credentials'. - `scope` is a comma-separated list of permissions you are requesting (e.g. 'view_collection'). You may use the resulting access token for all subsequent API calls which need user authentication. The access token should be provided in the HTTP header of the subsequent API calls according the following model: `Authorization: Bearer {access_token}` The access token has a limited validity period. The lifetime of the access token is indicated in the response of the API. Optional: grant_type (default: authorization_code), code, client_id (plus 3 more). Returns the OauthTokenGetResponse.

params.name: code
params.description: For grant type 'authorization_code', this is the authorization code (mandatory for grant type 'authorization_code')

params.name: client_id
params.description: For grant type 'authorization_code', client ID (mandatory for grant type 'authorization_code')

params.name: client_secret
params.description: For grant type 'authorization_code', client secret, which is the same as the API key (mandatory for grant type 'authorization_code')

params.name: redirect_uri
params.description: For grant type 'authorization_code', repeat the redirect URI used to get the authorization code (mandatory for grant type 'authorization_code')

params.name: scope
params.description: A comma-separated list of permissions you are requesting (e.g. 'view_collection') (for grant type 'client_credentials')

tools.name: publications_get
tools.description: Retrieve the details about a specific item in the literature catalogue. Required: id. Returns the Publication.

params.name: id
params.description: Numista ID of the publication to fetch, starting with 'L' followed by 6 digits.

tools.name: types_get
tools.description: Retrieve the details about a specific type in the catalogue. Required: type_id. Optional: lang (default: en). Returns the Type.

params.name: type_id
params.description: ID of the type to fetch

tools.name: types_search
tools.description: Search the catalogue for coin, banknote, and exonumia types. At least one of the following parameters should be provided: `q`, `issuer`, `catalogue`, `date`, or `year`. Optional: lang (default: en), q, object_type (plus 12 more). Returns the TypesSearchResponse.

params.name: object_type
params.description: ID of a object type. If provided, only the objects of this type are returned.

params.name: issuer
params.description: Issuer code. If provided, only the types from the given issuer are returned.

params.name: catalogue
params.description: ID of a reference catalogue. If provided, only the types referenced in the given catalogue are returned.

params.name: number
params.description: Number of the searched typed in a reference catalogue. This parameter works only with the other parameter `catalogue`.

params.name: ruler
params.description: ID of a ruler. If provided, only the types issued by this ruler are returned.

params.name: material
params.description: ID of a material. If provided, only the types made of this material are returned.

params.name: year
params.description: Year (for example '1302'), or a range of years (for example '1800-1850'), as written on the item.

params.name: date
params.description: Year of the Gregorian calendar when the type was issued, or a range of years (for example '1789', '800-900').

params.name: size
params.description: Diameter of the item (or largest dimension for non-round objects) in millimetres, or a range of diameters (for example '10.5', '9-11').

params.name: weight
params.description: Weight of the item in grams, or a range of weights (for example '31.1', '10-12').

tools.name: types_issues_get
tools.description: The types in the catalogue have one or more issues for the different years, mintmarks and varieties. Required: type_id. Optional: lang (default: en). Returns array of Issue.

params.name: type_id
params.description: ID of the type to fetch the issues from

tools.name: types_issues_get-prices
tools.description: Get estimates for the price of an issue, depending on the grade. Required: type_id, issue_id. Optional: currency (default: EUR), lang (default: en). Returns the IssuesGetPricesResponse.

tools.name: users_get
tools.description: Get details about a user. Required: user_id. Optional: lang (default: en). Returns the UsersGetResponse.

tools.name: users_collected-items_add
tools.description: This functionality requires the OAuth 2.0 authentication with the scope 'edit_collection'. Required: user_id, type. Optional: acquisition_date, acquisition_place, axis (plus 14 more). Returns the new CollectedItem.

params.name: for_swap
params.description: Indicate whether the item is available for swap

params.name: issue
params.description: ID of the issue of the item

params.name: type
params.description: ID of the type of the item

tools.name: users_collected-items_delete
tools.description: This functionality requires the OAuth 2.0 authentication with the scope 'edit_collection'. Required: user_id, item_id. Destructive.

tools.name: users_collected-items_edit
tools.description: All fields in the request body are optional. Only the fields which are present will be updated. This functionality requires the OAuth 2.0 authentication with the scope 'edit_collection'. Required: user_id, item_id. Optional: acquisition_date, acquisition_place, axis (plus 15 more). Returns the updated CollectedItem.

params.name: for_swap
params.description: Indicate whether the item is available for swap

params.name: grade
params.description: Grade of the item, or null for undetermined grade

params.name: issue
params.description: ID of the issue of the item, or null for undetermined issue

params.name: type
params.description: ID of the type of the item

tools.name: users_collected-items_get
tools.description: Retrieve the details about all the items (coins, banknotes, pieces of exonumia) in collected by the user. This functionality requires the OAuth 2.0 authentication with the scope 'view_collection'. Required: user_id. Optional: category, type, collection. Returns the CollectedItemsGetResponse.

params.name: category
params.description: Category. If this parameter is provided, only items of the given category are returned.

params.name: type
params.description: Type ID. If this parameter is provided, only items of the given type are returned.

params.name: collection
params.description: Collection ID. If this parameter is provided, only items in the given collection are returned.

tools.name: users_collected-items_get-users
tools.description: Retrieve the details about a specific item collected by the user. This functionality requires the OAuth 2.0 authentication with the scope 'view_collection'. Required: user_id, item_id. Returns the CollectedItem.

tools.name: users_collections_get-user
tools.description: A user can organize their items in multiple collections. This functionality requires the OAuth 2.0 authentication with the scope 'view_collection'. Required: user_id. Returns the CollectionsGetUserResponse.
